title: Design Patterns — Behavioral
learningObjectives: Defend **Behavioral design patterns** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why Design Patterns — Behavioral matters
sections.content: **Behavioral** patterns model **collaboration** and **variation**. Interviewers love **Strategy** over **growing** **switch** statements and **Command** for **queues** and **undo**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Design Patterns — Behavioral
sections.content:
### Day 80 — **Behavioral** **patterns**

### 1. Strategy
**Pluggable** **algorithms**.

### 2. Observer
**Pub/sub** **object** graph.

### 3. Command
**Requests** as **first-class** objects.

### 4. State
**Explicit** **transitions**.

### 5. Template Method
**Invariant** **flow**, **variant** **steps**.

### 6. Story
**Pricing** **rules** **explode**—**Strategy** **registry** + **Spring** **beans**.

sections.title: Basic — Behavioral headline
Intermediate — Strategy plug-in
sections.output: inject Strategy implementation instead of switch(type)

sections.title: Advanced — Command queue
sections.output: command bus: enqueue Command objects for workers

sections.title: Strategy context
Exercise — Design Patterns — Behavioral (Day 80 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: strategy: open for extension; if-else: edit risk

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Strategy** vs **if-else** **chains**?
conceptual.answer: Encapsulate varying algorithms behind interface—open for extension without editing core flow. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Behavioral design patterns**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Encapsulate varying algorithms behind interface—open for extension without editing core flow. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Encapsulate varying algorithms behind interface—open for extension without editing core fl Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

conceptual.question: **Observer** **push** vs **pull**?
conceptual.answer: Push sends events; pull lets observers query—memory and coupling trade-offs. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Behavioral design patterns**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Push sends events; pull lets observers query—memory and coupling trade-offs. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.answer: State transitions owned by context object graph; strategy is pluggable algorithm choice. conceptual.question: **Template** **Method** **hook**?
conceptual.answer: Fixed skeleton in base; subclasses override steps—watch fragile inheritance. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Design Patterns — Behavioral** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact** and **ETA** during this incident?
followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: Which **post-incident** **action** items are **non-negotiable**?
followUps.answer: At minimum: **runbook** update, **dashboard** gap closed, **test** that **reproduces** the failure, and **owner** with **deadline**. Tie themes to **error** **budget** policy. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Pattern | Recall |
|---|---|
| Strategy | Swap algorithm |
| Observer | Notify dependents |
| Command | Request object |
| State | Transition graph |
| Template | Skeleton method |
| Mediator | Reduce mesh |
| Memento | Snapshot state |
| Iterator | Hide traversal |
| Chain | Pass along |
| Anti | Mega switch |
